assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: Code Change Request' and 'Action: Create sub-task (subtask-30001.md)'. The report's Review Comments Summary table shows comment 30001 classified as 'Code change request' with 'Sub-task Created: Yes (subtask-30001.md)'. The file subtask-30001.md exists in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: Suggestion' and its Reasoning section explicitly identifies the suggestive language: 'Key phrases include "should also add" (additive suggestion, not a correction), "would help" (conditional benefit, not a requirement), and "Something like" (proposing one possible approach rather than dictating a specific fix).' The report table also shows comment 30002 classified as 'Suggestion'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: Nit' and 'Action: No sub-task created. Nit-level comments about internal error message strings do not warrant follow-up tasks.' The report's Review Comments Summary shows comment 30003 as 'Nit' with 'Sub-task Created: No'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: Question' and 'Action: No sub-task created. Questions requiring author/team clarification are not actionable code change requests.' The report's Review Comments Summary shows comment 30004 as 'Question' with 'Sub-task Created: No'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 3), '## Files to Modify' (line 7), '## Implementation Notes' (line 10), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 17).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 23) with the URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 24).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 26) with 'Review comment #30001 by reviewer-a' and quotes the full original comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...' (lines 27-29).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/reporting files: report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. All are markdown documentation files. No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report itself verifies scope containment noting 'No out-of-scope files modified'.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) are individually classified in separate review-*.md files and all four appear in the report's Review Comments Summary table. The report's findings section references comments 30001, 30002, 30003, and 30004, demonstrating all were processed before the report was generated.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's verdict is 'FAIL -- Changes Requested by Reviewer'. The report contains no merge commands, no gh pr merge invocations, and no indication that a merge was attempted or performed. The report only analyzes and creates sub-tasks.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's Domain Verification Summary table contains '| Style/Conventions | Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE |'. Additionally, the dedicated 'Test Change Classification' section states: 'Classification: ADDITIVE' and explains 'All test changes are in a new test file (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs). No existing test files were modified.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full '### Convention Upgrade Evaluation' subsection that examines: (1) repository structure showing CONVENTIONS.md exists but its content is unavailable, (2) Key Conventions from repo-backend.md listing conventions none of which mention database indexing, (3) no existing migration files demonstrating index patterns. Conclusion: 'There is no documented convention or demonstrated codebase pattern in the available fixture data that requires migrations adding filterable columns to also include indexes. Without such convention evidence, the suggestion cannot be upgraded.' The report table also shows '| Style/Conventions | Convention Upgrade | EVALUATED -- comment 30002 not upgraded (no convention backing) |'.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'Action: No sub-task created. Suggestions without convention backing do not warrant follow-up tasks.' The report table shows comment 30002 with 'Sub-task Created: No'. No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory (only subtask-30001.md exists). The convention upgrade evaluation in review-30002.md explicitly concludes no convention evidence supports an upgrade.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The report's Domain Verification Summary shows '| Style/Conventions | Eval Quality | N/A |'. The dedicated 'Eval Quality' section states: 'Classification: N/A' and explains: 'Eval quality assessment uses three criteria: (1) comment author is github-actions[bot], (2) comment body contains ## Eval Results, and (3) comment body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals. All three must match for a comment to be classified as an eval result review. No comments in this PR match these criteria.'
